behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Candidates: Integration (top) vs Unit (runner-up). Reject Unit because the behavior explicitly requires parse success to "proceed through normal hook input handling," which is a parser-to-handler handoff across modules. Reject System by evidence: behavior text does not require "real process execution," OS, network, or subprocess. Reject Agentic/Workflow by evidence: no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local parser + hook-input handling modules in-process. Feed valid JSON fixtures whose string fields contain code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse succeeds and downstream hook-input handling receives expected structured data without error traces.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Candidates: Integration (top) vs System (runner-up). Reject System because behavior only requires malformed payload -> trace record mapping, not "real process execution" or OS-level stdin semantics. Reject Unit because required output is a `json_parse_failed` trace record "with enough context," which typically combines parser error details and trace formatting/sink wiring (module boundary). Reject Agentic/Workflow by evidence: no model decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser + trace-record construction/sink adapter together in-process. Supply malformed JSON cases (unterminated string, missing comma, bad escape). Assert emitted trace type is `json_parse_failed` and context fields (offset/snippet/error message) identify malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Candidates: Unit (top) vs Integration (runner-up). Reject Integration because behavior is explicitly "local error-policy path" and focuses on mapping parse failure to handled result and telemetry intent flag, which can be validated at one object/function boundary. Reject higher levels by evidence: no requirement for real stdin/process/OS, and no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy function/object directly with a representative parse-failure error. Assert returned result is handled (no exception thrown), expected status is set, and telemetry-intent marker is true/present.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Candidates: System (top) vs Integration (runner-up). Reject Integration with direct evidence: behavior explicitly says "In real process execution" and references malformed "stdin" plus process not crashing before exit; this failure boundary is subprocess/OS I/O. Reject Agentic/Workflow by evidence: no model output dependency or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed bytes/JSON to stdin, close stdin, and wait for exit. Assert non-crash behavior (expected handled exit semantics) and verify expected parse-failure trace signal is emitted before process exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Candidates: System (top) vs Integration (runner-up). Reject Integration because behavior explicitly requires a "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)," which crosses real process/stdin behavior and end-to-end artifact emission. Reject Agentic/Workflow by evidence: no LLM/model step described.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke hook entrypoint the same way production does (real process with stdin). Feed malformed input, then inspect the real trace artifact destination (file/stdout/collector output) and assert artifact content/type matches parse-failure expectation.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Candidates: Integration (top) vs Unit (runner-up). Reject Unit because this behavior is comparative across two categories (malformed JSON vs valid markdown-heavy JSON) and requires attribution correctness across parse + classification/trace handling boundaries. Reject System by evidence: behavior does not require "real process execution"; in-process module wiring is sufficient. Reject Agentic/Workflow by evidence: no AI/ML judgment involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired regression fixtures: (a) valid JSON with heavy markdown strings, (b) truly malformed JSON. Run through in-process parser + failure-attribution/trace path and assert only malformed cases produce `json_parse_failed`, while valid-markdown cases do not, preventing false attribution.
